_doc: Cells the coverage matrix should show as N/A (not_expected) instead of 'missing': partitions whose report genuinely does NOT disclose a given statement (verified against the PDF — never used to hide an extraction gap for data that IS printed). statement='*' = every NON-CORE lane this partition is missing (core BS/PL/cross always stay flagged); statement may also be a single key or a LIST of keys. period and kind default to '*' (wildcard = every quarter / both consolidation bases) — use them for a bank that STRUCTURALLY never files a lane; period (and statement) may also be a LIST to scope a bank that omitted a lane only in some quarters. Entries are applied only when the cell is otherwise 'missing'.

not_disclosed.note: 14-page brief interim filing — none of the §4/footnote tables are disclosed
9-page brief consolidated filing — no §4/footnote tables (no capital/liquidity/etc. terms in the PDF)

Q1 interim abbreviates the credit-risk footnotes: no III/IV/V NPL-movement table and no IFRS-9 stage table (Stage 1/2/3 columns) anywhere — only prose references. FIBA discloses these tables only in Q2/Q4. (OCI/cash-flow ARE disclosed but on image-only statement pages — left flagged.)

Sector table uses the legacy pre-IFRS-9 schema (Değer Kaybına Uğramış / Tahsili Gecikmiş / Değer Ayarlamaları / Karşılıklar) — it does NOT break Stage-2/Stage-3 out by sector, so this Stage-by-sector lane is genuinely not disclosed. Extractor skips the page rather than fabricate stage values.
